title: Peloton Q3 Earnings Preview: 1 Key Metric You'll Want to Know
text: Peloton Q3 2021 Earnings Preview. Key Metric for Investors: Connected fitness subscriptions stand out as the most important metric to watch ahead of Peloton's Q3 earnings report on May 6, 2021. Q2 Performance: The company reported 1.67 million connected fitness subscriptions in Q2, representing strong growth. Analyst Expectations: Wall Street expected Q3 revenue of approximately $1.1 billion with an expected loss per share of $0.12. Forward Guidance: Management guided investors to expect 1.98 million connected fitness subscriptions in Q3. Strategic Importance: Connected fitness subscriptions reveal the company's earning potential over the long run because they indicate market penetration and growth potential. As subscription numbers increase, Peloton can spread its fixed content creation costs across more users, leading to improved profitability. Market Sentiment: Peloton stock had declined over 36% year-to-date prior to the earnings report as investors worried about demand destruction from gym reopenings and rising vaccination rates. However, the company's six consecutive years of at least 100% annual revenue growth even before the pandemic suggested underlying business strength.
